title: Rust Compiler Lints
description: Lint settings for Rust compiler individual lints and lint groups.

aarch64_softfloat_neon.title: Aarch64 Softfloat Neon
aarch64_softfloat_neon.description: The `aarch64_softfloat_neon` lint detects usage of `#[target_feature(enable = "neon")]` on softfloat aarch64 targets. Enabling this target feature causes LLVM to alter the ABI of function calls, making this attribute unsound to use.

absolute_paths_not_starting_with_crate.title: Absolute Paths Not Starting With Crate
absolute_paths_not_starting_with_crate.description: The `absolute_paths_not_starting_with_crate` lint detects fully qualified paths that start with a module name instead of `crate`, `self`, or an extern crate name

ambiguous_associated_items.title: Ambiguous Associated Items
ambiguous_associated_items.description: The `ambiguous_associated_items` lint detects ambiguity between associated items and enum variants.

ambiguous_glob_imports.title: Ambiguous Glob Imports
ambiguous_glob_imports.description: The `ambiguous_glob_imports` lint detects glob imports that should report ambiguity errors, but previously didn’t do that due to rustc bugs.

ambiguous_glob_reexports.title: Ambiguous Glob Reexports
ambiguous_glob_reexports.description: The `ambiguous_glob_reexports` lint detects cases where names re-exported via globs collide. Downstream users trying to use the same name re-exported from multiple globs will receive a warning pointing out redefinition of the same name.

ambiguous_negative_literals.title: Ambiguous Negative Literals
ambiguous_negative_literals.description: The `ambiguous_negative_literals` lint checks for cases that are confusing between a negative literal and a negation that’s not part of the literal.

ambiguous_wide_pointer_comparisons.title: Ambiguous Wide Pointer Comparisons
ambiguous_wide_pointer_comparisons.description: The `ambiguous_wide_pointer_comparisons` lint checks comparison of `*const/*mut ?Sized` as the operands.

anonymous_parameters.title: Anonymous Parameters
anonymous_parameters.description: The `anonymous_parameters` lint detects anonymous parameters in trait definitions.

arithmetic_overflow.title: Arithmetic Overflow
arithmetic_overflow.description: The `arithmetic_overflow` lint detects that an arithmetic operation will overflow.

array_into_iter.title: Array Into Iter
array_into_iter.description: The `array_into_iter` lint detects calling `into_iter` on arrays.

asm_sub_register.title: Asm Sub Register
asm_sub_register.description: The `asm_sub_register` lint detects using only a subset of a register for inline asm inputs.

async_fn_in_trait.title: Async Fn In Trait
async_fn_in_trait.description: The `async_fn_in_trait` lint detects use of `async fn` in the definition of a publicly-reachable trait.

bad_asm_style.title: Bad Asm Style
bad_asm_style.description: The `bad_asm_style` lint detects the use of the `.intel_syntax` and `.att_syntax` directives.

bare_trait_objects.title: Bare Trait Objects
bare_trait_objects.description: The `bare_trait_objects` lint suggests using `dyn Trait` for trait objects.

binary_asm_labels.title: Binary Asm Labels
binary_asm_labels.description: The `binary_asm_labels` lint detects the use of numeric labels containing only binary digits in the inline `asm!` macro.

bindings_with_variant_name.title: Bindings With Variant Name
bindings_with_variant_name.description: The `bindings_with_variant_name` lint detects pattern bindings with the same name as one of the matched variants.

boxed_slice_into_iter.title: Boxed Slice Into Iter
boxed_slice_into_iter.description: The `boxed_slice_into_iter` lint detects calling `into_iter` on boxed slices.

break_with_label_and_loop.title: Break With Label And Loop
break_with_label_and_loop.description: The `break_with_label_and_loop` lint detects labeled `break` expressions with an unlabeled loop as their value expression.

clashing_extern_declarations.title: Clashing Extern Declarations
clashing_extern_declarations.description: The `clashing_extern_declarations` lint detects when an `extern fn` has been declared with the same name but different types.

closure_returning_async_block.title: Closure Returning Async Block
closure_returning_async_block.description: The `closure_returning_async_block` lint detects cases where users write a closure that returns an async block.

coherence_leak_check.title: Coherence Leak Check
coherence_leak_check.description: The `coherence_leak_check` lint detects conflicting implementations of a trait that are only distinguished by the old leak-check code.

conflicting_repr_hints.title: Conflicting Repr Hints
conflicting_repr_hints.description: The `conflicting_repr_hints` lint detects `repr` attributes with conflicting hints.

confusable_idents.title: Confusable Idents
confusable_idents.description: The `confusable_idents` lint detects visually confusable pairs between identifiers.

const_evaluatable_unchecked.title: Const Evaluatable Unchecked
const_evaluatable_unchecked.description: The `const_evaluatable_unchecked` lint detects a generic constant used in a type.

const_item_mutation.title: Const Item Mutation
const_item_mutation.description: The `const_item_mutation` lint detects attempts to mutate a `const` item.

dangerous_implicit_autorefs.title: Dangerous Implicit Autorefs
dangerous_implicit_autorefs.description: The `dangerous_implicit_autorefs` lint checks for implicitly taken references to dereferences of raw pointers.

dangling_pointers_from_temporaries.title: Dangling Pointers From Temporaries
dangling_pointers_from_temporaries.description: The `dangling_pointers_from_temporaries` lint detects getting a pointer to data of a temporary that will immediately get dropped.

dead_code.title: Dead Code
dead_code.description: The `dead_code` lint detects unused, unexported items.

default_overrides_default_fields.title: Default Overrides Default Fields
default_overrides_default_fields.description: The `default_overrides_default_fields` lint checks for manual `impl` blocks of the `Default` trait of types with default field values.

dependency_on_unit_never_type_fallback.title: Dependency On Unit Never Type Fallback
dependency_on_unit_never_type_fallback.description: The `dependency_on_unit_never_type_fallback` lint detects cases where code compiles with never type fallback being `()`, but will stop compiling with fallback being `!`.

deprecated.title: Deprecated
deprecated.description: The `deprecated` lint detects use of deprecated items.

deprecated_in_future.title: Deprecated In Future
deprecated_in_future.description: detects use of items that will be deprecated in a future version.

deprecated_safe_2024.title: Deprecated Safe 2024
deprecated_safe_2024.description: The `deprecated_safe_2024` lint detects unsafe functions being used as safe functions.

deprecated_where_clause_location.title: Deprecated Where Clause Location
deprecated_where_clause_location.description: The `deprecated_where_clause_location` lint detects when a where clause in front of the equals in an associated type.

deref_into_dyn_supertrait.title: Deref Into Dyn Supertrait
deref_into_dyn_supertrait.description: The `deref_into_dyn_supertrait` lint is emitted whenever there is a `Deref` implementation for `dyn SubTrait` with a `dyn SuperTrait` type as the `Output` type.

deref_nullptr.title: Deref Nullptr
deref_nullptr.description: The `deref_nullptr` lint detects when a null pointer is dereferenced, which causes undefined behavior.

double_negations.title: Double Negations
double_negations.description: The `double_negations` lint detects expressions of the form `--x`.

drop_bounds.title: Drop Bounds
drop_bounds.description: The `drop_bounds` lint checks for generics with `std::ops::Drop` as bounds.

dropping_copy_types.title: Dropping Copy Types
dropping_copy_types.description: The `dropping_copy_types` lint checks for calls to `std::mem::drop` with a value that derives the Copy trait.

dropping_references.title: Dropping References
dropping_references.description: The `dropping_references` lint checks for calls to `std::mem::drop` with a reference instead of an owned value.

duplicate_macro_attributes.title: Duplicate Macro Attributes
duplicate_macro_attributes.description: The `duplicate_macro_attributes` lint detects when a `#[test]`-like built-in macro attribute is duplicated on an item. This lint may trigger on `bench`, `cfg_eval`, `test` and `test_case`.

dyn_drop.title: Dyn Drop
dyn_drop.description: The `dyn_drop` lint checks for trait objects with `std::ops::Drop`.

edition_2024_expr_fragment_specifier.title: Edition 2024 Expr Fragment Specifier
edition_2024_expr_fragment_specifier.description: The `edition_2024_expr_fragment_specifier` lint detects the use of `expr` fragments in macros during migration to the 2024 edition.

elided_lifetimes_in_associated_constant.title: Elided Lifetimes In Associated Constant
elided_lifetimes_in_associated_constant.description: The `elided_lifetimes_in_associated_constant` lint detects elided lifetimes in associated constants when there are other lifetimes in scope. This was accidentally supported, and this lint was later relaxed to allow eliding lifetimes to `'static` when there are no lifetimes in scope.

elided_lifetimes_in_paths.title: Elided Lifetimes In Paths
elided_lifetimes_in_paths.description: The `elided_lifetimes_in_paths` lint detects the use of hidden lifetime parameters.

ellipsis_inclusive_range_patterns.title: Ellipsis Inclusive Range Patterns
ellipsis_inclusive_range_patterns.description: The `ellipsis_inclusive_range_patterns` lint detects the `...` range pattern, which is deprecated.

enum_intrinsics_non_enums.title: Enum Intrinsics Non Enums
enum_intrinsics_non_enums.description: The `enum_intrinsics_non_enums` lint detects calls to intrinsic functions that require an enum (`core::mem::discriminant`, `core::mem::variant_count`), but are called with a non-enum type.

explicit_builtin_cfgs_in_flags.title: Explicit Builtin Cfgs In Flags
explicit_builtin_cfgs_in_flags.description: The `explicit_builtin_cfgs_in_flags` lint detects builtin cfgs set via the `--cfg` flag.

explicit_outlives_requirements.title: Explicit Outlives Requirements
explicit_outlives_requirements.description: The `explicit_outlives_requirements` lint detects unnecessary lifetime bounds that can be inferred.

exported_private_dependencies.title: Exported Private Dependencies
exported_private_dependencies.description: The `exported_private_dependencies` lint detects private dependencies that are exposed in a public interface.

ffi_unwind_calls.title: Ffi Unwind Calls
ffi_unwind_calls.description: The `ffi_unwind_calls` lint detects calls to foreign functions or function pointers with `C-unwind` or other FFI-unwind ABIs.

for_loops_over_fallibles.title: For Loops Over Fallibles
for_loops_over_fallibles.description: The `for_loops_over_fallibles` lint checks for `for` loops over `Option` or `Result` values.

forbidden_lint_groups.title: Forbidden Lint Groups
forbidden_lint_groups.description: The `forbidden_lint_groups` lint detects violations of `forbid` applied to a lint group. Due to a bug in the compiler, these used to be overlooked entirely. They now generate a warning.

forgetting_copy_types.title: Forgetting Copy Types
forgetting_copy_types.description: The `forgetting_copy_types` lint checks for calls to `std::mem::forget` with a value that derives the Copy trait.

forgetting_references.title: Forgetting References
forgetting_references.description: The `forgetting_references` lint checks for calls to `std::mem::forget` with a reference instead of an owned value.

function_item_references.title: Function Item References
function_item_references.description: The `function_item_references` lint detects function references that are formatted with `fmt::Pointer` or transmuted.

fuzzy_provenance_casts.title: Fuzzy Provenance Casts
fuzzy_provenance_casts.description: The `fuzzy_provenance_casts` lint detects an `as` cast between an integer and a pointer.

hidden_glob_reexports.title: Hidden Glob Reexports
hidden_glob_reexports.description: The `hidden_glob_reexports` lint detects cases where glob re-export items are shadowed by private items.

if_let_rescope.title: If Let Rescope
if_let_rescope.description: The `if_let_rescope` lint detects cases where a temporary value with significant drop is generated on the right hand side of `if let` and suggests a rewrite into `match` when possible.

ill_formed_attribute_input.title: Ill Formed Attribute Input
ill_formed_attribute_input.description: The `ill_formed_attribute_input` lint detects ill-formed attribute inputs that were previously accepted and used in practice.

impl_trait_overcaptures.title: Impl Trait Overcaptures
impl_trait_overcaptures.description: The `impl_trait_overcaptures` lint warns against cases where lifetime capture behavior will differ in edition 2024.

impl_trait_redundant_captures.title: Impl Trait Redundant Captures
impl_trait_redundant_captures.description: The `impl_trait_redundant_captures` lint warns against cases where use of the precise capturing `use ` syntax is not needed.

improper_ctypes.title: Improper Ctypes
improper_ctypes.description: The `improper_ctypes` lint detects incorrect use of types in foreign modules.

improper_ctypes_definitions.title: Improper Ctypes Definitions
improper_ctypes_definitions.description: The `improper_ctypes_definitions` lint detects incorrect use of `extern` function definitions.

incomplete_features.title: Incomplete Features
incomplete_features.description: The `incomplete_features` lint detects unstable features enabled with the `feature` attribute that may function improperly in some or all cases.

incomplete_include.title: Incomplete Include
incomplete_include.description: The `incomplete_include` lint detects the use of the `include!` macro with a file that contains more than one expression.

ineffective_unstable_trait_impl.title: Ineffective Unstable Trait Impl
ineffective_unstable_trait_impl.description: The `ineffective_unstable_trait_impl` lint detects `#[unstable]` attributes which are not used.

inline_no_sanitize.title: Inline No Sanitize
inline_no_sanitize.description: The `inline_no_sanitize` lint detects incompatible use of `#[inline(always)]` and `#[sanitize(xyz = "off")]`.

internal_features.title: Internal Features
internal_features.description: The `internal_features` lint detects unstable features enabled with the `feature` attribute that are internal to the compiler or standard library.

invalid_atomic_ordering.title: Invalid Atomic Ordering
invalid_atomic_ordering.description: The `invalid_atomic_ordering` lint detects passing an `Ordering` to an atomic operation that does not support that ordering.

invalid_doc_attributes.title: Invalid Doc Attributes
invalid_doc_attributes.description: The `invalid_doc_attributes` lint detects when the `#[doc(...)]` is misused.

invalid_from_utf8.title: Invalid From Utf8
invalid_from_utf8.description: The `invalid_from_utf8` lint checks for calls to `std::str::from_utf8` and `std::str::from_utf8_mut` with a known invalid UTF-8 value.

invalid_from_utf8_unchecked.title: Invalid From Utf8 Unchecked
invalid_from_utf8_unchecked.description: The `invalid_from_utf8_unchecked` lint checks for calls to `std::str::from_utf8_unchecked` and `std::str::from_utf8_unchecked_mut` with a known invalid UTF-8 value.

invalid_macro_export_arguments.title: Invalid Macro Export Arguments
invalid_macro_export_arguments.description: The `invalid_macro_export_arguments` lint detects cases where `#[macro_export]` is being used with invalid arguments.

invalid_nan_comparisons.title: Invalid Nan Comparisons
invalid_nan_comparisons.description: The `invalid_nan_comparisons` lint checks comparison with `f32::NAN` or `f64::NAN` as one of the operand.

invalid_null_arguments.title: Invalid Null Arguments
invalid_null_arguments.description: The `invalid_null_arguments` lint checks for invalid usage of null pointers in arguments.

invalid_reference_casting.title: Invalid Reference Casting
invalid_reference_casting.description: The `invalid_reference_casting` lint checks for casts of `&T` to `&mut T` without using interior mutability.

invalid_type_param_default.title: Invalid Type Param Default
invalid_type_param_default.description: The `invalid_type_param_default` lint detects type parameter defaults erroneously allowed in an invalid location.

invalid_value.title: Invalid Value
invalid_value.description: The `invalid_value` lint detects creating a value that is not valid, such as a null reference.

irrefutable_let_patterns.title: Irrefutable Let Patterns
irrefutable_let_patterns.description: The `irrefutable_let_patterns` lint detects irrefutable patterns in `if let`s, `while let`s, and `if let` guards.

keyword_idents_2018.title: Keyword Idents 2018
keyword_idents_2018.description: The `keyword_idents_2018` lint detects edition keywords being used as an identifier.

keyword_idents_2024.title: Keyword Idents 2024
keyword_idents_2024.description: The `keyword_idents_2024` lint detects edition keywords being used as an identifier.

large_assignments.title: Large Assignments
large_assignments.description: The `large_assignments` lint detects when objects of large types are being moved around.

late_bound_lifetime_arguments.title: Late Bound Lifetime Arguments
late_bound_lifetime_arguments.description: The `late_bound_lifetime_arguments` lint detects generic lifetime arguments in path segments with late bound lifetime parameters.

legacy_derive_helpers.title: Legacy Derive Helpers
legacy_derive_helpers.description: The `legacy_derive_helpers` lint detects derive helper attributes that are used before they are introduced.

let_underscore_drop.title: Let Underscore Drop
let_underscore_drop.description: The `let_underscore_drop` lint checks for statements which don’t bind an expression which has a non-trivial Drop implementation to anything, causing the expression to be dropped immediately instead of at end of scope.

let_underscore_lock.title: Let Underscore Lock
let_underscore_lock.description: The `let_underscore_lock` lint checks for statements which don’t bind a mutex to anything, causing the lock to be released immediately instead of at end of scope, which is typically incorrect.

linker_messages.title: Linker Messages
linker_messages.description: The `linker_messages` lint forwards warnings from the linker.

long_running_const_eval.title: Long Running Const Eval
long_running_const_eval.description: The `long_running_const_eval` lint is emitted when const eval is running for a long time to ensure rustc terminates even if you accidentally wrote an infinite loop.

lossy_provenance_casts.title: Lossy Provenance Casts
lossy_provenance_casts.description: The `lossy_provenance_casts` lint detects an `as` cast between a pointer and an integer.

macro_expanded_macro_exports_accessed_by.title: Macro Expanded Macro Exports Accessed By Absolute Paths
macro_expanded_macro_exports_accessed_by.description: The `macro_expanded_macro_exports_accessed_by_absolute_paths` lint detects macro-expanded `macro_export` macros from the current crate that cannot be referred to by absolute paths.

macro_use_extern_crate.title: Macro Use Extern Crate
macro_use_extern_crate.description: The `macro_use_extern_crate` lint detects the use of the `macro_use` attribute.

malformed_diagnostic_attributes.title: Malformed Diagnostic Attributes
malformed_diagnostic_attributes.description: The `malformed_diagnostic_attributes` lint detects malformed diagnostic attributes.

malformed_diagnostic_format_literals.title: Malformed Diagnostic Format Literals
malformed_diagnostic_format_literals.description: The `malformed_diagnostic_format_literals` lint detects malformed diagnostic format literals.

map_unit_fn.title: Map Unit Fn
map_unit_fn.description: The `map_unit_fn` lint checks for `Iterator::map` receive a callable that returns `()`.

meta_variable_misuse.title: Meta Variable Misuse
meta_variable_misuse.description: The `meta_variable_misuse` lint detects possible meta-variable misuse in macro definitions.

mismatched_lifetime_syntaxes.title: Mismatched Lifetime Syntaxes
mismatched_lifetime_syntaxes.description: The `mismatched_lifetime_syntaxes` lint detects when the same lifetime is referred to by different syntaxes between function arguments and return values.

misplaced_diagnostic_attributes.title: Misplaced Diagnostic Attributes
misplaced_diagnostic_attributes.description: The `misplaced_diagnostic_attributes` lint detects wrongly placed diagnostic attributes.

missing_abi.title: Missing Abi
missing_abi.description: The `missing_abi` lint detects cases where the ABI is omitted from `extern` declarations.

missing_copy_implementations.title: Missing Copy Implementations
missing_copy_implementations.description: The `missing_copy_implementations` lint detects potentially-forgotten implementations of `Copy` for public types.

missing_debug_implementations.title: Missing Debug Implementations
missing_debug_implementations.description: The `missing_debug_implementations` lint detects missing implementations of `fmt::Debug` for public types.

missing_docs.title: Missing Docs
missing_docs.description: The `missing_docs` lint detects missing documentation for public items.

missing_unsafe_on_extern.title: Missing Unsafe On Extern
missing_unsafe_on_extern.description: The `missing_unsafe_on_extern` lint detects missing unsafe keyword on extern declarations.

mixed_script_confusables.title: Mixed Script Confusables
mixed_script_confusables.description: The `mixed_script_confusables` lint detects visually confusable characters in identifiers between different scripts.

multiple_supertrait_upcastable.title: Multiple Supertrait Upcastable
multiple_supertrait_upcastable.description: The `multiple_supertrait_upcastable` lint detects when a dyn-compatible trait has multiple supertraits.

must_not_suspend.title: Must Not Suspend
must_not_suspend.description: The `must_not_suspend` lint guards against values that shouldn’t be held across suspend points (`.await`)

mutable_transmutes.title: Mutable Transmutes
mutable_transmutes.description: The `mutable_transmutes` lint catches transmuting from `&T` to `&mut T` because it is undefined behavior.

named_arguments_used_positionally.title: Named Arguments Used Positionally
named_arguments_used_positionally.description: The `named_arguments_used_positionally` lint detects cases where named arguments are only used positionally in format strings. This usage is valid but potentially very confusing.

named_asm_labels.title: Named Asm Labels
named_asm_labels.description: The `named_asm_labels` lint detects the use of named labels in the inline `asm!` macro.

never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe.title: Never Type Fallback Flowing Into Unsafe
never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe.description: The `never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe` lint detects cases where never type fallback affects unsafe function calls.

no_mangle_const_items.title: No Mangle Const Items
no_mangle_const_items.description: The `no_mangle_const_items` lint detects any `const` items with the `no_mangle` attribute.

no_mangle_generic_items.title: No Mangle Generic Items
no_mangle_generic_items.description: The `no_mangle_generic_items` lint detects generic items that must be mangled.

non_camel_case_types.title: Non Camel Case Types
non_camel_case_types.description: The `non_camel_case_types` lint detects types, variants, traits and type parameters that don’t have camel case names.

non_contiguous_range_endpoints.title: Non Contiguous Range Endpoints
non_contiguous_range_endpoints.description: The `non_contiguous_range_endpoints` lint detects likely off-by-one errors when using exclusive range patterns.

non_exhaustive_omitted_patterns.title: Non Exhaustive Omitted Patterns
non_exhaustive_omitted_patterns.description: The `non_exhaustive_omitted_patterns` lint aims to help consumers of a `#[non_exhaustive]` struct or enum who want to match all of its fields/variants explicitly.

non_fmt_panics.title: Non Fmt Panics
non_fmt_panics.description: The `non_fmt_panics` lint detects `panic!(..)` invocations where the first argument is not a formatting string.

non_local_definitions.title: Non Local Definitions
non_local_definitions.description: The `non_local_definitions` lint checks for `impl` blocks and `#[macro_export]` macro inside bodies (functions, enum discriminant, …).

non_shorthand_field_patterns.title: Non Shorthand Field Patterns
non_shorthand_field_patterns.description: The `non_shorthand_field_patterns` lint detects using `Struct { x: x }` instead of `Struct { x }` in a pattern.

non_snake_case.title: Non Snake Case
non_snake_case.description: The `non_snake_case` lint detects variables, methods, functions, lifetime parameters and modules that don’t have snake case names.

non_upper_case_globals.title: Non Upper Case Globals
non_upper_case_globals.description: The `non_upper_case_globals` lint detects static items that don’t have uppercase identifiers.

noop_method_call.title: Noop Method Call
noop_method_call.description: The `noop_method_call` lint detects specific calls to noop methods such as a calling ` ::clone` where `T: !Clone`.

opaque_hidden_inferred_bound.title: Opaque Hidden Inferred Bound
opaque_hidden_inferred_bound.description: The `opaque_hidden_inferred_bound` lint detects cases in which nested `impl Trait` in associated type bounds are not written generally enough to satisfy the bounds of the associated type.

out_of_scope_macro_calls.title: Out Of Scope Macro Calls
out_of_scope_macro_calls.description: The `out_of_scope_macro_calls` lint detects `macro_rules` called when they are not in scope, above their definition, which may happen in key-value attributes.

overflowing_literals.title: Overflowing Literals
overflowing_literals.description: The `overflowing_literals` lint detects literals out of range for their type.

overlapping_range_endpoints.title: Overlapping Range Endpoints
overlapping_range_endpoints.description: The `overlapping_range_endpoints` lint detects `match` arms that have range patterns that overlap on their endpoints.

path_statements.title: Path Statements
path_statements.description: The `path_statements` lint detects path statements with no effect.

patterns_in_fns_without_body.title: Patterns In Fns Without Body
patterns_in_fns_without_body.description: The `patterns_in_fns_without_body` lint detects `mut` identifier patterns as a parameter in functions without a body.

private_bounds.title: Private Bounds
private_bounds.description: The `private_bounds` lint detects types in a secondary interface of an item, that are more private than the item itself. Secondary interface of an item consists of bounds on generic parameters and where clauses, including supertraits for trait items.

private_interfaces.title: Private Interfaces
private_interfaces.description: The `private_interfaces` lint detects types in a primary interface of an item, that are more private than the item itself. Primary interface of an item is all its interface except for bounds on generic parameters and where clauses.

proc_macro_derive_resolution_fallback.title: Proc Macro Derive Resolution Fallback
proc_macro_derive_resolution_fallback.description: The `proc_macro_derive_resolution_fallback` lint detects proc macro derives using inaccessible names from parent modules.

ptr_to_integer_transmute_in_consts.title: Ptr To Integer Transmute In Consts
ptr_to_integer_transmute_in_consts.description: The `ptr_to_integer_transmute_in_consts` lint detects pointer to integer transmute in const functions and associated constants.

pub_use_of_private_extern_crate.title: Pub Use Of Private Extern Crate
pub_use_of_private_extern_crate.description: The `pub_use_of_private_extern_crate` lint detects a specific situation of re-exporting a private `extern crate`.

redundant_imports.title: Redundant Imports
redundant_imports.description: The `redundant_imports` lint detects imports that are redundant due to being imported already; either through a previous import, or being present in the prelude.

redundant_lifetimes.title: Redundant Lifetimes
redundant_lifetimes.description: The `redundant_lifetimes` lint detects lifetime parameters that are redundant because they are equal to another named lifetime.

redundant_semicolons.title: Redundant Semicolons
redundant_semicolons.description: The `redundant_semicolons` lint detects unnecessary trailing semicolons.

refining_impl_trait_internal.title: Refining Impl Trait Internal
refining_impl_trait_internal.description: The `refining_impl_trait_internal` lint detects `impl Trait` return types in method signatures that are refined by a trait implementation, meaning the implementation adds information about the return type that is not present in the trait.

refining_impl_trait_reachable.title: Refining Impl Trait Reachable
refining_impl_trait_reachable.description: The `refining_impl_trait_reachable` lint detects `impl Trait` return types in method signatures that are refined by a publicly reachable trait implementation, meaning the implementation adds information about the return type that is not present in the trait.

renamed_and_removed_lints.title: Renamed And Removed Lints
renamed_and_removed_lints.description: The `renamed_and_removed_lints` lint detects lints that have been renamed or removed.

repr_transparent_external_private_fields.title: Repr Transparent External Private Fields
repr_transparent_external_private_fields.description: transparent type contains an external ZST that is marked #[non_exhaustive] or contains private fields.

rust_2021_incompatible_closure_captures.title: Rust 2021 Incompatible Closure Captures
rust_2021_incompatible_closure_captures.description: The `rust_2021_incompatible_closure_captures` lint detects variables that aren’t completely captured in Rust 2021, such that the `Drop` order of their fields may differ between Rust 2018 and 2021.

rust_2021_incompatible_or_patterns.title: Rust 2021 Incompatible Or Patterns
rust_2021_incompatible_or_patterns.description: The `rust_2021_incompatible_or_patterns` lint detects usage of old versions of or-patterns.

rust_2021_prefixes_incompatible_syntax.title: Rust 2021 Prefixes Incompatible Syntax
rust_2021_prefixes_incompatible_syntax.description: The `rust_2021_prefixes_incompatible_syntax` lint detects identifiers that will be parsed as a prefix instead in Rust 2021.

rust_2021_prelude_collisions.title: Rust 2021 Prelude Collisions
rust_2021_prelude_collisions.description: The `rust_2021_prelude_collisions` lint detects the usage of trait methods which are ambiguous with traits added to the prelude in future editions.

rust_2024_guarded_string_incompatible_sy.title: Rust 2024 Guarded String Incompatible Syntax
rust_2024_guarded_string_incompatible_sy.description: The `rust_2024_guarded_string_incompatible_syntax` lint detects `#` tokens that will be parsed as part of a guarded string literal in Rust 2024.

rust_2024_incompatible_pat.title: Rust 2024 Incompatible Pat
rust_2024_incompatible_pat.description: The `rust_2024_incompatible_pat` lint detects patterns whose meaning will change in the Rust 2024 edition.

rust_2024_prelude_collisions.title: Rust 2024 Prelude Collisions
rust_2024_prelude_collisions.description: The `rust_2024_prelude_collisions` lint detects the usage of trait methods which are ambiguous with traits added to the prelude in future editions.

self_constructor_from_outer_item.title: Self Constructor From Outer Item
self_constructor_from_outer_item.description: The `self_constructor_from_outer_item` lint detects cases where the `Self` constructor was silently allowed due to a bug in the resolver, and which may produce surprising and unintended behavior.

semicolon_in_expressions_from_macros.title: Semicolon In Expressions From Macros
semicolon_in_expressions_from_macros.description: The `semicolon_in_expressions_from_macros` lint detects trailing semicolons in macro bodies when the macro is invoked in expression position. This was previous accepted, but is being phased out.

single_use_lifetimes.title: Single Use Lifetimes
single_use_lifetimes.description: The `single_use_lifetimes` lint detects lifetimes that are only used once.

soft_unstable.title: Soft Unstable
soft_unstable.description: The `soft_unstable` lint detects unstable features that were unintentionally allowed on stable. This is a future-incompatible lint to transition this to a hard error in the future. See issue #64266 for more details.

special_module_name.title: Special Module Name
special_module_name.description: The `special_module_name` lint detects module declarations for files that have a special meaning.

stable_features.title: Stable Features
stable_features.description: The `stable_features` lint detects a `feature` attribute that has since been made stable.

static_mut_refs.title: Static Mut Refs
static_mut_refs.description: The `static_mut_refs` lint checks for shared or mutable references of mutable static inside `unsafe` blocks and `unsafe` functions.

supertrait_item_shadowing_definition.title: Supertrait Item Shadowing Definition
supertrait_item_shadowing_definition.description: detects when a supertrait item is shadowed by a subtrait item.

supertrait_item_shadowing_usage.title: Supertrait Item Shadowing Usage
supertrait_item_shadowing_usage.description: detects when a supertrait item is shadowed by a subtrait item.

suspicious_double_ref_op.title: Suspicious Double Ref Op
suspicious_double_ref_op.description: The `suspicious_double_ref_op` lint checks for usage of `.clone()`/`.borrow()`/`.deref()` on an `&&T` when `T: !Deref/Borrow/Clone`, which means the call will return the inner `&T`, instead of performing the operation on the underlying `T` and can be confusing.

tail_expr_drop_order.title: Tail Expr Drop Order
tail_expr_drop_order.description: The `tail_expr_drop_order` lint looks for those values generated at the tail expression location, that runs a custom `Drop` destructor. Some of them may be dropped earlier in Edition 2024 that they used to in Edition 2021 and prior. This lint detects those cases and provides you information on those values and their custom destructor implementations. Your discretion on this information is required.

test_unstable_lint.title: Test Unstable Lint
test_unstable_lint.description: The `test_unstable_lint` lint tests unstable lints and is perma-unstable.

text_direction_codepoint_in_comment.title: Text Direction Codepoint In Comment
text_direction_codepoint_in_comment.description: The `text_direction_codepoint_in_comment` lint detects Unicode codepoints in comments that change the visual representation of text on screen in a way that does not correspond to their on memory representation.

text_direction_codepoint_in_literal.title: Text Direction Codepoint In Literal
text_direction_codepoint_in_literal.description: The `text_direction_codepoint_in_literal` lint detects Unicode codepoints that change the visual representation of text on screen in a way that does not correspond to their on memory representation.

trivial_bounds.title: Trivial Bounds
trivial_bounds.description: The `trivial_bounds` lint detects trait bounds that don’t depend on any type parameters.

trivial_casts.title: Trivial Casts
trivial_casts.description: The `trivial_casts` lint detects trivial casts which could be replaced with coercion, which may require a temporary variable.

trivial_numeric_casts.title: Trivial Numeric Casts
trivial_numeric_casts.description: The `trivial_numeric_casts` lint detects trivial numeric casts of types which could be removed.

type_alias_bounds.title: Type Alias Bounds
type_alias_bounds.description: The `type_alias_bounds` lint detects bounds in type aliases.

tyvar_behind_raw_pointer.title: Tyvar Behind Raw Pointer
tyvar_behind_raw_pointer.description: The `tyvar_behind_raw_pointer` lint detects raw pointer to an inference variable.

uncommon_codepoints.title: Uncommon Codepoints
uncommon_codepoints.description: The `uncommon_codepoints` lint detects uncommon Unicode codepoints in identifiers.

unconditional_panic.title: Unconditional Panic
unconditional_panic.description: The `unconditional_panic` lint detects an operation that will cause a panic at runtime.

unconditional_recursion.title: Unconditional Recursion
unconditional_recursion.description: The `unconditional_recursion` lint detects functions that cannot return without calling themselves.

uncovered_param_in_projection.title: Uncovered Param In Projection
uncovered_param_in_projection.description: The `uncovered_param_in_projection` lint detects a violation of one of Rust’s orphan rules for foreign trait implementations that concerns the use of type parameters inside trait associated type paths (“projections”) whose output may not be a local type that is mistakenly considered to “cover” said parameters which is unsound and which may be rejected by a future version of the compiler.

undropped_manually_drops.title: Undropped Manually Drops
undropped_manually_drops.description: The `undropped_manually_drops` lint check for calls to `std::mem::drop` with a value of `std::mem::ManuallyDrop` which doesn’t drop.

unexpected_cfgs.title: Unexpected Cfgs
unexpected_cfgs.description: The `unexpected_cfgs` lint detects unexpected conditional compilation conditions.

unfulfilled_lint_expectations.title: Unfulfilled Lint Expectations
unfulfilled_lint_expectations.description: The `unfulfilled_lint_expectations` lint detects when a lint expectation is unfulfilled.

ungated_async_fn_track_caller.title: Ungated Async Fn Track Caller
ungated_async_fn_track_caller.description: The `ungated_async_fn_track_caller` lint warns when the `#[track_caller]` attribute is used on an async function without enabling the corresponding unstable feature flag.

unit_bindings.title: Unit Bindings
unit_bindings.description: The `unit_bindings` lint detects cases where bindings are useless because they have the unit type `()` as their inferred type. The lint is suppressed if the user explicitly annotates the let binding with the unit type `()`, or if the let binding uses an underscore wildcard pattern, i.e. `let _ = expr`, or if the binding is produced from macro expansions.

unknown_crate_types.title: Unknown Crate Types
unknown_crate_types.description: The `unknown_crate_types` lint detects an unknown crate type found in a `crate_type` attribute.

unknown_diagnostic_attributes.title: Unknown Diagnostic Attributes
unknown_diagnostic_attributes.description: The `unknown_diagnostic_attributes` lint detects unknown diagnostic attributes.

unknown_lints.title: Unknown Lints
unknown_lints.description: The `unknown_lints` lint detects unrecognized lint attributes.

unnameable_test_items.title: Unnameable Test Items
unnameable_test_items.description: The `unnameable_test_items` lint detects `#[test]` functions that are not able to be run by the test harness because they are in a position where they are not nameable.

unnameable_types.title: Unnameable Types
unnameable_types.description: The `unnameable_types` lint detects types for which you can get objects of that type, but cannot name the type itself.

unnecessary_transmutes.title: Unnecessary Transmutes
unnecessary_transmutes.description: The `unnecessary_transmutes` lint detects transmutations that have safer alternatives.

unpredictable_function_pointer_compariso.title: Unpredictable Function Pointer Comparisons
unpredictable_function_pointer_compariso.description: The `unpredictable_function_pointer_comparisons` lint checks comparison of function pointer as the operands.

unqualified_local_imports.title: Unqualified Local Imports
unqualified_local_imports.description: The `unqualified_local_imports` lint checks for `use` items that import a local item using a path that does not start with `self::`, `super::`, or `crate::`.

unreachable_code.title: Unreachable Code
unreachable_code.description: The `unreachable_code` lint detects unreachable code paths.

unreachable_pub.title: Unreachable Pub
unreachable_pub.description: The `unreachable_pub` lint triggers for `pub` items not reachable from other crates - that means neither directly accessible, nor reexported (with `pub use`), nor leaked through things like return types (which the `unnameable_types` lint can detect if desired).

unsafe_attr_outside_unsafe.title: Unsafe Attr Outside Unsafe
unsafe_attr_outside_unsafe.description: The `unsafe_attr_outside_unsafe` lint detects a missing unsafe keyword on attributes considered unsafe.

unsafe_code.title: Unsafe Code
unsafe_code.description: The `unsafe_code` lint catches usage of `unsafe` code and other potentially unsound constructs like `no_mangle`, `export_name`, and `link_section`.

unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn.title: Unsafe Op In Unsafe Fn
unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn.description: The `unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn` lint detects unsafe operations in unsafe functions without an explicit unsafe block.

unstable_name_collisions.title: Unstable Name Collisions
unstable_name_collisions.description: The `unstable_name_collisions` lint detects that you have used a name that the standard library plans to add in the future.

unstable_syntax_pre_expansion.title: Unstable Syntax Pre Expansion
unstable_syntax_pre_expansion.description: The `unstable_syntax_pre_expansion` lint detects the use of unstable syntax that is discarded during attribute expansion.

unsupported_calling_conventions.title: Unsupported Calling Conventions
unsupported_calling_conventions.description: The `unsupported_calling_conventions` lint is output whenever there is a use of the `stdcall`, `fastcall`, and `cdecl` calling conventions (or their unwind variants) on targets that cannot meaningfully be supported for the requested target.

unused_allocation.title: Unused Allocation
unused_allocation.description: The `unused_allocation` lint detects unnecessary allocations that can be eliminated.

unused_assignments.title: Unused Assignments
unused_assignments.description: The `unused_assignments` lint detects assignments that will never be read.

unused_associated_type_bounds.title: Unused Associated Type Bounds
unused_associated_type_bounds.description: The `unused_associated_type_bounds` lint is emitted when an associated type bound is added to a trait object, but the associated type has a `where Self: Sized` bound, and is thus unavailable on the trait object anyway.

unused_attributes.title: Unused Attributes
unused_attributes.description: The `unused_attributes` lint detects attributes that were not used by the compiler.

unused_braces.title: Unused Braces
unused_braces.description: The `unused_braces` lint detects unnecessary braces around an expression.

unused_comparisons.title: Unused Comparisons
unused_comparisons.description: The `unused_comparisons` lint detects comparisons made useless by limits of the types involved.

unused_crate_dependencies.title: Unused Crate Dependencies
unused_crate_dependencies.description: The `unused_crate_dependencies` lint detects crate dependencies that are never used.

unused_doc_comments.title: Unused Doc Comments
unused_doc_comments.description: The `unused_doc_comments` lint detects doc comments that aren’t used by `rustdoc`.

unused_extern_crates.title: Unused Extern Crates
unused_extern_crates.description: The `unused_extern_crates` lint guards against `extern crate` items that are never used.

unused_features.title: Unused Features
unused_features.description: The `unused_features` lint detects unused or unknown features found in crate-level `feature` attributes.

unused_import_braces.title: Unused Import Braces
unused_import_braces.description: The `unused_import_braces` lint catches unnecessary braces around an imported item.

unused_imports.title: Unused Imports
unused_imports.description: The `unused_imports` lint detects imports that are never used.

unused_labels.title: Unused Labels
unused_labels.description: The `unused_labels` lint detects labels that are never used.

unused_lifetimes.title: Unused Lifetimes
unused_lifetimes.description: The `unused_lifetimes` lint detects lifetime parameters that are never used.

unused_macro_rules.title: Unused Macro Rules
unused_macro_rules.description: The `unused_macro_rules` lint detects macro rules that were not used.

unused_macros.title: Unused Macros
unused_macros.description: The `unused_macros` lint detects macros that were not used.

unused_must_use.title: Unused Must Use
unused_must_use.description: The `unused_must_use` lint detects unused result of a type flagged as `#[must_use]`.

unused_mut.title: Unused Mut
unused_mut.description: The `unused_mut` lint detects mut variables which don’t need to be mutable.

unused_parens.title: Unused Parens
unused_parens.description: The `unused_parens` lint detects `if`, `match`, `while` and `return` with parentheses; they do not need them.

unused_qualifications.title: Unused Qualifications
unused_qualifications.description: The `unused_qualifications` lint detects unnecessarily qualified names.

unused_results.title: Unused Results
unused_results.description: The `unused_results` lint checks for the unused result of an expression in a statement.

unused_unsafe.title: Unused Unsafe
unused_unsafe.description: The `unused_unsafe` lint detects unnecessary use of an `unsafe` block.

unused_variables.title: Unused Variables
unused_variables.description: The `unused_variables` lint detects variables which are not used in any way.

useless_deprecated.title: Useless Deprecated
useless_deprecated.description: The `useless_deprecated` lint detects deprecation attributes with no effect.

useless_ptr_null_checks.title: Useless Ptr Null Checks
useless_ptr_null_checks.description: The `useless_ptr_null_checks` lint checks for useless null checks against pointers obtained from non-null types.

uses_power_alignment.title: Uses Power Alignment
uses_power_alignment.description: The `uses_power_alignment` lint detects specific `repr(C)` aggregates on AIX. In its platform C ABI, AIX uses the “power” (as in PowerPC) alignment rule (detailed in https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/xl-c-and-cpp-aix/16.1?topic=data-using-alignment-modes#alignment), which can also be set for XLC by `#pragma align(power)` or `-qalign=power`. Aggregates with a floating-point type as the recursively first field (as in “at offset 0”) modify the layout of subsequent fields of the associated structs to use an alignment value where the floating-point type is aligned on a 4-byte boundary.

variant_size_differences.title: Variant Size Differences
variant_size_differences.description: The `variant_size_differences` lint detects enums with widely varying variant sizes.

warnings.title: Warnings
warnings.description: All lints that are set to issue warnings

deprecated-safe.title: Deprecated Safe
deprecated-safe.description: Lints for functions which were erroneously marked as safe in the past

future-incompatible.title: Future Incompatible
future-incompatible.description: Lints that detect code that has future-compatibility problems

keyword-idents.title: Keyword Idents
keyword-idents.description: Lints that detect identifiers which will be come keywords in later editions

let-underscore.title: Let Underscore
let-underscore.description: Lints that detect wildcard let bindings that are likely to be invalid

refining-impl-trait.title: Refining Impl Trait
refining-impl-trait.description: Detects refinement of `impl Trait` return types by trait implementations

rust-2018-compatibility.title: Rust 2018 Compatibility
rust-2018-compatibility.description: Lints used to transition code from the 2015 edition to 2018

rust-2018-idioms.title: Rust 2018 Idioms
rust-2018-idioms.description: Lints to nudge you toward idiomatic features of Rust 2018

rust-2021-compatibility.title: Rust 2021 Compatibility
rust-2021-compatibility.description: Lints used to transition code from the 2018 edition to 2021

rust-2024-compatibility.title: Rust 2024 Compatibility
rust-2024-compatibility.description: Lints used to transition code from the 2021 edition to 2024

unknown-or-malformed-diagnostic-attribut.title: Unknown Or Malformed Diagnostic Attributes
unknown-or-malformed-diagnostic-attribut.description: detects unknown or malformed diagnostic attributes

unused.title: Unused
unused.description: Lints that detect things being declared but not used, or excess syntax

priority.description: The priority that controls which lints or [lint groups](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/lints/groups.html) override other lint groups. Lower (particularly negative) numbers have lower priority, being overridden by higher numbers, and show up first on the command-line to tools like rustc.

check-cfg.description: A list of `cfg` expressions that this lint should check for.

LintLevel.title: Lint Level
LintLevel.description: Specify the [lint level](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/lints/levels.html) for a lint or lint group.
